content:
8 min read
6 hours ago

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

My AI assistant generated a perfect component on Monday.
By Wednesday it had silently shipped a zone.js pattern into my zoneless app. No error. No warning. The component rendered, the data loaded, and the screen never updated after the first load.
That bug took me two hours to find. The AI wrote it in four seconds.
This is the reality of AI-assisted Angular development in 2026. The tools are extraordinary and the traps are new. I spent one full week building exclusively with AI assistance, measuring what it accelerated, cataloguing what it broke, and learning the one setup that changed the quality of AI output from “dangerously outdated” to “genuinely useful.”
That setup is the Angular MCP server. Most Angular developers have not heard of it yet. By the end of this article, you will have it running, and you will understand exactly why it exists.
The Problem: Your AI Does Not Know Your Angular
Ask any AI coding assistant to generate an Angular component today without additional context. Here is what you will likely receive:
import { Component, OnInit } from '@angular/core';
import {…
